questions.q: A trader marks his goods 40% above cost price and allows a discount of 25%. His gain percent is:
The average of first 50 natural numbers is:
questions.explanation: Average of first n natural numbers = (n+1)/2 = 51/2 = 25.5.

questions.q: A sum becomes ₹2916 in 2 years at compound interest of 8% per annum. What is the principal?
If the selling price of 12 articles is equal to the cost price of 16 articles, what is the gain percent?
questions.explanation: SP of 12 = CP of 16. Let CP = 1 each. SP per article = 16/12 = 4/3. Gain% = (4/3−1)×100 = 33.33%.

questions.q: The perimeter of an equilateral triangle is 72 cm. What is its area?
Speed of a boat in still water is 10 km/h and speed of stream is 2 km/h. Time taken to go 48 km upstream is:
questions.explanation: Upstream speed = 10−2 = 8 km/h. Time = 48/8 = 6 hours.

questions.q: If 20% of A = 50% of B, then A:B is:
A can complete a work in 10 days and B in 15 days. They work together for 3 days and then A leaves. In how many more days will B finish the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Together per day = 1/10+1/15 = 1/6. In 3 days done = 1/2. Remaining = 1/2. B finishes in (1/2)/(1/15) = 7.5 ≈ 9? Recalc: 1/2 ÷ 1/15 = 15/2 = 7.5. Nearest option is 9 but actual = 7.5 days.

questions.q: What is the value of (1+tan²θ)?
questions.explanation: Trigonometric identity: 1 + tan²θ = sec²θ.

questions.q: The HCF and LCM of two numbers are 12 and 144 respectively. If one number is 36, find the other.
questions.explanation: Product of numbers = HCF × LCM = 12 × 144 = 1728. Other number = 1728/36 = 48.

questions.q: A number when increased by 20% gives 480. What is the number?
If the diagonal of a square is 8√2 cm, what is its area?
questions.explanation: Diagonal = side × √2 → 8√2 = side × √2 → side = 8. Area = 8² = 64 cm².

questions.q: By selling 45 pens a shopkeeper gains the cost price of 9 pens. What is his gain percent?
questions.explanation: Gain = CP of 9 pens. For 45 pens: SP − CP = CP of 9 → SP = CP of 54 pens. Gain% = 9/45 × 100 = 20%.

questions.q: Two numbers are in ratio 3:4. Their LCM is 48. What is their sum?
questions.explanation: Numbers = 3k and 4k. LCM = 12k = 48 → k = 4. Numbers = 12, 16. Sum = 28. Wait: LCM(3k,4k) = 12k = 48 → k=4 → 12+16=28. Correct answer is 28.

questions.q: The compound interest on ₹8000 at 10% per annum for 1.5 years, compounded half-yearly is:
A rectangular room is 15 m long and 12 m wide. What is the cost of carpeting it at ₹35 per sq m?
Three numbers are in the ratio 2:3:4. If their sum is 54, the largest number is:
questions.explanation: Sum of ratio = 9. Largest = 4/9 × 54 = 24.

questions.q: A man invests ₹5000 at 4% per annum simple interest. After how many years will the amount be ₹6000?
The value of √(0.0625) is:
If 4x − 3y = 19 and 3x + 2y = 17, what is (x+y)?
A train running at 54 km/h crosses a platform in 30 seconds. If the platform is 150 m long, what is the length of the train?
questions.explanation: Speed = 54×5/18 = 15 m/s. Distance = 15×30 = 450 m. Length of train = 450−150 = 300 m.

questions.q: The volume of a cube is 1000 cm³. Its total surface area is:
What is 15% of 1/3 of 900?
If a person walks at 15 km/h instead of 10 km/h, he would have walked 25 km more in the same time. What is the actual distance travelled?
questions.explanation: Time = same. 15T − 10T = 25 → 5T = 25 → T = 5 hrs. Actual distance = 10×5 = 50 km.

questions.q: The sum of squares of two consecutive even numbers is 340. Find the numbers.
In the series: 5, 11, 23, 47, 95, ?, find the next number.
Find the odd one out: 16, 25, 36, 72, 144
questions.explanation: 16=4², 25=5², 36=6², 144=12². 72 is not a perfect square.

questions.q: If CLOCK is coded as KCOLC, how is LOTUS coded?
questions.explanation: CLOCK reversed = KCOLC. Similarly LOTUS reversed = SUTOL.

questions.q: Monika is the sister-in-law of Deepak. Suman is the brother of Monika. How is Deepak related to Suman?
questions.explanation: Monika is sister-in-law of Deepak → Deepak married Monika's sibling, OR Deepak's sibling is Monika. Suman is brother of Monika. If Deepak is husband of Monika's sister-in-law... Deepak is married to Suman's sister → Deepak is Suman's brother-in-law.

questions.q: Suresh walks 10 m towards North, then 6 m towards East, then 10 m towards South. How far is he from start and in which direction?
questions.explanation: North 10 then South 10 = net 0. East 6 m. So he is 6 m East of starting point.

questions.q: Arrange the following in meaningful order: 1.Infant 2.Child 3.Adult 4.Old 5.Teenager
questions.explanation: Chronological life stages: Infant(1) → Child(2) → Teenager(5) → Adult(3) → Old(4). Order: 1,2,5,3,4.

questions.q: Which number will replace the question mark? 4:64::5:?
questions.explanation: 4³ = 64. Similarly 5³ = 125.

questions.q: If SUGAR = 23, MILK = 11, then what is the value of WATER?
questions.explanation: SUGAR: 5 letters, S+U+G+A+R positional sum. Method: letter count → 5+4=9? No. SUGAR=5 letters, positions 19+21+7+1+18=66/3≈22? Alternate: number of letters: SUGAR=5, 5+18(R position logic). Standard: SUGAR=5 letters→23=5+18; MILK=4 letters→11=4+7; WATER=5 letters→5+19=24.

questions.q: Statements: All cats are dogs. No dog is a rat. Conclusions: I. No cat is a rat. II. All dogs are cats.
questions.explanation: All cats are dogs + No dog is a rat → No cat is a rat (I follows). All dogs are cats does not follow from 'all cats are dogs'. So only I follows.

questions.q: How many triangles are in a triangle divided by 3 medians?
questions.explanation: 3 medians of a triangle divide it into 6 smaller triangles of equal area.

questions.q: A's position from top in a class of 40 is 9th. B is 3 ranks below A. What is B's position from bottom?
questions.explanation: A = 9th from top. B = 12th from top. B from bottom = 40−12+1 = 29th.

questions.q: Select the synonym of DILIGENT:
questions.explanation: Diligent means having or showing care and conscientiousness in one's work. Synonym: Hardworking.

questions.q: Select the antonym of ASCEND:
questions.explanation: Ascend means to go up or climb. Its antonym is Descend — to go down.

questions.q: Identify the error: 'The news are very disturbing.'
questions.explanation: 'News' is an uncountable noun and always takes a singular verb. Correct: 'The news is very disturbing.'

questions.q: Fill in the blank: 'Despite working hard, she failed to _____ her goal.'
questions.explanation: 'To' is followed by base form of verb. 'to achieve' is correct.

questions.q: Choose the correctly spelt word:
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is 'Calendar' — a chart or series of pages showing the days, weeks, and months of a year.

questions.q: Select the one-word substitute for: 'One who studies insects':
questions.explanation: An entomologist is a scientist who studies insects. Ornithologist studies birds.

questions.q: Choose the word nearest in meaning to OBSTINATE:
questions.explanation: Obstinate means stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion. Synonym: Stubborn.

questions.q: Active to Passive: 'The manager has signed the contract.'
options: The contract was signed by the manager.
The contract has been signed by the manager.
The contract had been signed by the manager.
The contract is signed by the manager.
questions.explanation: Present perfect active → Present perfect passive: has/have + been + past participle. 'The contract has been signed by the manager.'

questions.q: Direct to Indirect: The teacher said to the students, 'Work hard and you will succeed.'
options: The teacher told the students to work hard and that they would succeed.
The teacher asked the students to work hard and that they would succeed.
The teacher advised the students to work hard and said that they would succeed.
The teacher commanded the students to work hard and would succeed.
questions.explanation: Imperative + result in Direct Speech → advised + to + V and said that they would + V in Indirect.

questions.q: Identify the figure of speech: 'The wind whispered through the trees.'
questions.explanation: 'Whispered' is a human action attributed to the wind (non-human), making this Personification.

questions.q: Fill in the blank with the correct preposition: 'She is very good _____ mathematics.'
questions.explanation: The correct preposition with 'good' when referring to a subject or skill is 'good at'.

questions.q: Which part of the sentence has an error? 'If I would have worked harder, I would have passed.'
questions.explanation: In conditional sentences (Type 3), the 'if clause' uses past perfect: 'had worked', not 'would have worked'. Correct: 'If I had worked harder, I would have passed.'

questions.q: Who was the last Viceroy of India?
questions.explanation: Lord Mountbatten was the last Viceroy of British India (February–August 1947) and became the first Governor-General of independent India.

questions.q: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution are borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV) were borrowed from the Irish Constitution (Ireland).

questions.q: Which is the highest civilian award in India?
questions.explanation: Bharat Ratna is the highest civilian honour in India, instituted in 1954.

questions.q: The National Song of India, 'Vande Mataram', was composed by:
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' was composed by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay and is from his novel 'Anandamath' (1882).

questions.q: The Tropic of Cancer does NOT pass through which of the following states?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 states: Gujarat, Rajasthan, MP, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Tripura, and Mizoram. Maharashtra is not among them.

questions.q: What is the full form of 'GST' in the Indian economic context?
questions.explanation: GST stands for Goods and Services Tax — a comprehensive, multi-stage, destination-based tax implemented in India on 1 July 2017.

questions.q: Which Article of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability and its practice in any form is forbidden.

questions.q: Light Year is a unit of:
questions.explanation: A light year is the distance that light travels in one year (approximately 9.46 × 10¹² km). It is a unit of distance.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a fossil fuel?
questions.explanation: Wood is a biomass fuel, not a fossil fuel. Coal, petroleum, and natural gas are fossil fuels formed from remains of ancient organisms.

questions.q: Who was the founder of the Indian National Army (INA)?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Army was founded by Captain Mohan Singh in 1942 in Singapore. Subhas Chandra Bose later led and reorganized it.

questions.q: The 'Quit India Movement' was launched in the year:
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement (August Kranti) was launched by Mahatma Gandhi on 8 August 1942 at the Bombay session of the Congress.

questions.q: Which vitamin is produced when skin is exposed to sunlight?
questions.explanation: Vitamin D is synthesized in the skin when it is exposed to ultraviolet (UV) radiation from the sun.

questions.q: The headquarters of ISRO is located at:
questions.explanation: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is headquartered in Bengaluru (Bangalore), Karnataka.

questions.q: Which article of the Constitution deals with the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression?
questions.explanation: Article 19(1)(a) guarantees the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression as a Fundamental Right.

questions.q: Which is India's first indigenously developed vaccine against COVID-19?
questions.explanation: Covaxin was India's first indigenously developed COVID-19 vaccine, developed by Bharat Biotech in collaboration with ICMR.

questions.q: The Chipko Movement was related to:
questions.explanation: The Chipko Movement (1970s) in the Garhwal Himalayas was a forest conservation movement where villagers hugged trees to prevent them from being felled.

questions.q: The 'Sangam Age' is associated with the history of:
questions.explanation: The Sangam Age refers to the ancient period of Tamil literature (300 BCE to 300 CE) in South India.

questions.q: India's first space mission to Mars was named:
questions.explanation: India's Mars Orbiter Mission (MOM), popularly called Mangalyaan, was launched in November 2013 and entered Mars orbit in September 2014.

questions.q: Who has the power to issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights?
options: Both Supreme Court and High Courts
questions.explanation: Under Article 32, the Supreme Court can issue writs, and under Article 226, the High Courts can also issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

questions.q: The largest continent by area is:
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent in the world, covering approximately 44.58 million km² (30% of Earth's total land area).

questions.q: Rani Lakshmi Bai was the Queen of:
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmi Bai was the Queen of Jhansi and one of the leading figures of the Revolt of 1857.

questions.q: Which of the following is a Kharif crop?
questions.explanation: Rice is a Kharif crop sown during the monsoon season (June–July) and harvested in autumn. Wheat, Mustard, and Barley are Rabi crops.

questions.q: Who is the author of 'Arthashastra'?
questions.explanation: Arthashastra is an ancient Indian treatise on statecraft, economic policy and military strategy written by Chanakya (also known as Kautilya or Vishnugupta).

questions.q: The main component of LPG (Liquefied Petroleum Gas) is:
questions.explanation: LPG is mainly composed of Butane and Propane (hydrocarbon gases) stored under pressure in liquid form.

questions.q: 'Dandi March' (Salt March) took place in the year:
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Dandi March (Salt Satyagraha) from 12 March to 6 April 1930, covering 241 miles from Sabarmati Ashram to Dandi, Gujarat.

questions.q: The Parliament of India consists of:
options: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha only
President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Vice President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Prime Minister, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: Under Article 79, the Parliament of India consists of the President, the Rajya Sabha (Council of States) and the Lok Sabha (House of the People).

questions.q: Which of the following elements is liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature (and standard pressure). Bromine is the only non-metal liquid at room temperature.

questions.q: The winner of the Nobel Peace Prize 2023 was:
questions.explanation: Narges Mohammadi, an Iranian women's rights activist, was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize 2023 for her fight against the oppression of women in Iran.

questions.q: India hosted the G20 Summit in September 2023 under which theme?
questions.explanation: India's G20 2023 Presidency theme was 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam — One Earth, One Family, One Future', from the Maha Upanishad.

questions.q: Chandrayaan-3 became the first mission to land near the Moon's south pole on:
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-3's Vikram lander successfully touched down on the Moon's south polar region on 23 August 2023, making India the first country to achieve this.

questions.q: India became the world's most populous country surpassing China in:
questions.explanation: According to the UN, India surpassed China to become the world's most populous country in 2023 with a population of over 1.428 billion.

questions.q: The Women's Reservation Bill (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) provides what percentage of reservation for women in Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Constitution (106th Amendment) Act, 2023 provides 33% (one-third) reservation for women in the Lok Sabha, State Legislative Assemblies, and the National Capital Territory of Delhi.

questions.q: India's Aditya-L1 solar mission was launched to study the Sun from which Lagrange point?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1, launched on 2 September 2023, was placed in a halo orbit around the Sun-Earth Lagrange Point 1 (L1) to continuously observe the Sun without any eclipses.

questions.q: Which Indian player became the first to win the Chess World Championship in 2024?
questions.explanation: D. Gukesh became the youngest Chess World Champion in December 2024, defeating Ding Liren of China at the age of 18.

questions.q: 'One Nation One Election' concept in India proposes simultaneous elections for:
questions.explanation: 'One Nation One Election' proposes holding elections for the Lok Sabha and all State Legislative Assemblies simultaneously to reduce cost and time of frequent elections.

questions.q: India's first quantum computing mission was announced under which programme?
questions.explanation: The National Quantum Mission (NQM) was approved by the Government of India in April 2023 to develop quantum technology with a budget of ₹6003 crore over 8 years.

questions.q: Which country won the most medals at Paris Olympics 2024?
questions.explanation: The United States topped the Paris Olympics 2024 medal tally with 126 total medals including 40 gold medals.

questions.q: RBI's digital currency 'e-Rupee' (CBDC) was first launched as a pilot in:
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India launched the pilot of the Digital Rupee (e₹) — Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) — in November 2022, first in the wholesale segment.

questions.q: India's UPI (Unified Payments Interface) was launched by which organisation?
questions.explanation: UPI was developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) and launched in April 2016.

questions.q: The 'PM Vishwakarma' scheme was launched to support which group?
questions.explanation: PM Vishwakarma Scheme was launched on 17 September 2023 to provide support to traditional artisans and craftspeople (Vishwakarmas) across India with skill training, credit, and market access.
